name: Handling
purpose: Evasion, maneuvering, and repositioning. The Pilot's primary Power die for defensive and movement actions.

impairment.impaired: Power die steps down 1 size. Ship responds sluggishly — turns are wide, reactions delayed.
impairment.debilitated: Power die steps down 2 sizes. Ship barely maneuvers — drifting, overcorrecting, or locked into a heading.
impairment.offline: System unavailable. Ship cannot change heading or execute evasive actions. Pilot actions that require Handling automatically fail.

designNotes: Handling is the ship's agility stat. Impairing Handling makes the ship a sitting target — Evade rolls lose their Power die, Attack Runs lose positioning advantage.
Handling is paired with Engines for Pilot actions. Handling governs precision (evasion, threading gaps), Engines govern raw speed (pursuit, escape, closing distance).

name: Engines
purpose: Speed, pursuit, escape, and repositioning at range. The Pilot's Power die for speed-based actions and the Engineer's secondary Power die for rerouting.

impairment.impaired: Power die steps down 1 size. Ship loses acceleration — pursuit and escape become difficult.
impairment.debilitated: Power die steps down 2 sizes. Ship is crawling — cannot close distance or break away without assistance.
impairment.offline: System unavailable. Ship is dead in space. No pursuit, no escape, no repositioning. Hyperspace is impossible.

designNotes: Engines are the ship's speed stat. Impairing Engines turns a chase into a siege — the ship cannot run and cannot close.
Engines are the Engineer's primary reroute target — they can siphon engine power to boost other systems, or vice versa.

name: Shields
purpose: Damage absorption and proactive defense. The Engineer's primary Power die for Reinforce Shields and the ship's passive Endure defense.

impairment.impaired: Power die steps down 1 size. Shield coverage is patchy — hits that should be absorbed start getting through.
impairment.debilitated: Power die steps down 2 sizes. Shields are flickering — minimal absorption, catastrophic gaps in coverage.
impairment.offline: System unavailable. No shield absorption. Endure defense is unavailable — all incoming damage lands at full effect.

designNotes: Shields are the ship's toughness stat. They mirror personal armor's Endure bonus — stepping up the Power die when absorbing hits.
Shields offline is the starship equivalent of being unarmored. The crew feels every hit at full force. Engineers should prioritize shield repair above almost everything else.

name: Sensors
purpose: Scanning, jamming, targeting data, and electronic warfare. The Operator's primary Power die and the Co-Pilot's secondary Power die.

impairment.impaired: Power die steps down 1 size. Sensor data is degraded — scans are incomplete, targeting data is unreliable.
impairment.debilitated: Power die steps down 2 sizes. Sensors are barely functional — the crew is flying half-blind.
impairment.offline: System unavailable. No scanning, no electronic warfare, no targeting data. The ship is blind. Operator actions that require Sensors automatically fail. Resist defense is unavailable.

designNotes: Sensors are the ship's awareness stat. Impairing Sensors degrades the Operator and Co-Pilot simultaneously — a well-placed Called Shot to Sensors cripples the ship's information warfare.
Sensors offline means the crew cannot Scan, Jam, or Paint Target. The Gunner loses targeting data buffs. The Co-Pilot loses coordination tools. This is the electronic equivalent of [Blinded].

name: Fire Control
purpose: Offensive firepower management. A single Power die representing the ship's fire control computer, targeting systems, and weapon power distribution. The Gunner's Power die for all weapon attacks regardless of which mount they fire.

impairment.impaired: Power die steps down 1 size. Fire control solutions are degraded — targeting is sluggish, power distribution uneven.
impairment.debilitated: Power die steps down 2 sizes. Fire control barely functional — manual targeting, intermittent weapon power.
impairment.offline: System unavailable. No fire control — weapons cannot acquire targets. All Gunner attack actions automatically fail.

designNotes: Fire Control is a single system die for all weapons. Individual weapons define chassis (damage table), arc, range, and traits — but the Power die comes from Fire Control, not the weapon itself.

Impairing Fire Control degrades ALL weapon output simultaneously. This is intentional — a single Called Shot to Fire Control is the most efficient way to reduce a ship's offensive threat. The crew must choose between absorbing damage elsewhere or watching all their guns weaken.
Weapons still track their own status independently (a disabled mount is a disabled mount), but the Power die that drives them all is Fire Control.
